key_finding: M. oryzae trehalose-6-phosphate synthase TPS1 controls glucose-6-P partitioning and is required for full virulence; spore trehalose reserve is mobilized during appressorial development
M. grisea conidial glycogen + lipid + trehalose reserves are mobilized via PKA + MAP-kinase signalling during appressorial turgor generation -- foundational appressorial-metabolism paper
Colletotrichum graminicola appressoria generate ~5.5 MPa turgor via glycerol accumulation -- direct Colletotrichum measurement
M. grisea appressorial turgor (~8 MPa) is driven by glycerol accumulation -- foundational glycerol-as-osmolyte paper for appressorial fungi

Colletotrichum orbiculare Atg26-mediated pexophagy is required for appressorial host invasion; peroxisomes mobilize lipid-body fatty acids -- direct Colletotrichum evidence for peroxisomal β-oxidation during infection
M. oryzae peroxisomal multifunctional enzyme MFP1 knockouts (β-ox cycle steps 2+3) abolish virulence -- closest mechanistic homolog showing peroxisomal β-ox is required for appressorial penetration
Colletotrichum orbiculare requires peroxisomal lipid-body mobilization for appressorial penetration; oleate is the dominant TAG-fatty-acid in fungal lipid bodies
M. oryzae peroxisomal β-oxidation knockouts abolish virulence; oleate is the principal long-chain FA mobilized from conidial lipid bodies
M. grisea lipid-body TAG (oleate-rich in fungal spore lipidomes) mobilization during appressorial development; PKA + MAP-kinase regulated
C. higginsianum + C. graminicola in planta transcriptomes show cutinase + acyl-CoA oxidase + long-chain FA β-oxidation genes up-regulated specifically at the appressorial / penetration stage
C. graminicola on maize -- transcriptomic shift between appressorial / biotrophic / necrotrophic phases including cuticle-degrading enzymes at penetration
Fungal cutinase + cuticle-wax catabolism is the canonical entry strategy for cuticular-penetrating phytopathogens; VLCFA cleavage routes are part of the appressorial program
C. higginsianum + C. graminicola in planta transcriptome: hexose transporter genes up-regulated specifically during biotrophic primary-hypha stage as the fungus accesses apoplastic sugars
Uromyces fabae HXT1 hexose transporter localized to the haustorial / biotrophic plant-fungus interface -- canonical fungal-biotroph sugar-uptake paper
C. higginsianum + C. graminicola in planta sugar transporter expression during biotrophic phase; sucrose-converting invertases up-regulated at penetration
Fungal biotrophs use specialized sugar transporters at the haustorial / biotrophic interface to mine plant apoplastic carbohydrate -- direct fungal-pathogen sugar-uptake evidence

Colletotrichum lindemuthianum CLNR1 (nitrogen response regulator) controls global AA + N-source utilization including Glu/Gln catabolism; clnr1 deletion attenuates virulence on bean -- direct Colletotrichum AA metabolism + pathogenicity link
Cladosporium fulvum on tomato: apoplastic AA composition during biotrophic infection -- Glu + Gln dominant (>50% of free apoplastic AAs); direct fungal-phytopathogen apoplast nutrient measurement
C. lindemuthianum CLNR1 nitrogen-response regulator required for full virulence; controls AA permease + glutamine catabolism in planta -- direct Colletotrichum
Gln is the most abundant apoplastic AA in many host plants; fungal phytopathogens deamidate Gln to feed NH4+ and α-ketoglutarate
GABA is induced as a plant-defense AA upon pathogen attack; phytopathogens that re-route GABA via the fungal GABA shunt (GABA → succinic semialdehyde → succinate → TCA) bypass the defense
C. higginsianum / C. graminicola in planta transcriptomes show nitrogen-metabolism + GABA-shunt-relevant genes up-regulated during the biotrophic phase
Comparative genomics of C. sublineola and C. graminicola: Cs encodes extensive pectin-degrading CAZymes (polygalacturonases, pectate lyases, pectinesterases) -- direct C. sublineola CAZyme inventory

Aspergillus niger GaaA / GaaB / GaaC / GaaD (the fungal D-galacturonate Ashwell pathway: galU → L-galactonate → KDG → pyruvate + L-glyceraldehyde) -- canonical fungal galU-degradation mechanism, the pathway gap-filled into V10
C. higginsianum + C. graminicola in planta: pectinase + galU-degradation genes massively up-regulated during necrotrophic / late phase as host cell walls are dismantled
C. sublineola encodes a full xylanase + xylose-utilization repertoire; arabinoxylan is the dominant cereal hemicellulose Cs must degrade on sorghum

short_citation: de Vries & Visser 2001 Microbiol Mol Biol Rev 65:497
key_finding: Fungal xylanolytic systems + intracellular D-xylose catabolism (XR/XDH/XK pathway); canonical fungal-phytopathogen hemicellulose-utilization review
C. higginsianum / C. graminicola in planta xylanase up-regulation during necrotrophic phase
C. sublineola CAZyme repertoire includes α-L-arabinofuranosidases (release L-arabinose from arabinoxylan side chains); cereal-host adaptation

short_citation: Penttilä et al. 2007 Appl Environ Microbiol 73:7330
key_finding: Fungal L-arabinose catabolism via the Penttilä pathway (L-Ara → L-arabitol → L-xylulose → xylitol → D-xylulose-5P → PPP) demonstrated in Trichoderma reesei; this is exactly the pathway we gap-filled into V10
C. higginsianum / C. graminicola arabinofuranosidase + arabinoxylan-degrading genes up-regulated during necrotrophic colonisation
C. sublineola encodes α-amylase + glucoamylase genes for starch hydrolysis -- maltose is the immediate breakdown product accessible during necrotrophic invasion of starch-rich host tissue
C. higginsianum / C. graminicola amylase + α-glucosidase up-regulated during necrotrophic phase as host starch reserves become accessible
C. lindemuthianum CLNR1 GATA-type transcription factor controls broad AA + N-source assimilation; required for full virulence on bean -- direct Colletotrichum AA-uptake-and-pathogenicity link
C. higginsianum / C. graminicola broad AA permease + intracellular catabolic enzyme up-regulation during necrotrophic phase; reflects cytoplasmic AA pool release after host cell lysis
Necrotrophic phytopathogens access the host cytoplasmic AA pool released by cell lysis as a major mixed C+N source; nutritional switch reviewed
Pre-infection / appressorial spore-stage metabolism: simultaneous mobilization of trehalose + glycogen + lipid-body TAG (oleate-rich) during appressorial development; M. grisea
Combined lipid-body β-oxidation + glyoxylate cycle requirement for appressorial virulence -- the mixed-substrate baseline of the pre-infection state
Direct Colletotrichum evidence for the appressorial peroxisomal lipid-mobilization program (C. orbiculare)
C. higginsianum / C. graminicola in planta transcriptome: biotrophic-phase up-regulation of multiple sugar + AA + organic-acid transporters reflects the mixed apoplastic carbon environment
C. lindemuthianum CLNR1 control of AA + N-source uptake during biotrophic interaction with bean -- direct Colletotrichum biotrophic-phase nutrition regulator
Apoplastic carbon-source cocktail (sugars + AAs + organic acids) that biotrophic phytopathogens encounter -- review of the biotrophic nutritional environment
C. sublineola CAZyme repertoire: pectinases + cellulases + xylanases + arabinofuranosidases + amylases all encoded -- the enzymatic basis for the broad substrate mix released during necrotrophy
C. higginsianum / C. graminicola necrotrophic transition: massive up-regulation of plant cell-wall-degrading enzymes; broad polymer-breakdown product mix becomes available
Anthracnose disease biology of C. sublineola on Sorghum bicolor: necrotrophic colonization releases host cytoplasm and host-cell-wall polymers as broad mixed substrates -- direct Cs-sorghum reference
